key_points: The poem 'Somebody’s Mother' is written by Mary Dow Brine
The poem describes an old woman standing alone at a busy street crossing in winter
A group of boys pass by the woman but most of them ignore her situation
One kind boy notices her difficulty and decides to help her cross the street
The poem highlights the importance of kindness, empathy, and respect for the elderly
examples: The old woman is afraid to cross the snowy street alone
Most of the boys continue playing and do not notice her problem
One boy offers his arm and guides the woman safely across the road
The boy imagines that the woman could be someone’s mother
The old woman feels grateful and silently prays for the boy
formulas_or_processes: Situation → An old woman is unable to cross the busy street
Observation → A kind boy notices her difficulty
Action → The boy helps the woman cross the street safely
Result → The woman feels grateful and blessed
Message → Kindness and empathy make society better
exam_tips: Remember that Mary Dow Brine wrote the poem
Understand why the old woman was afraid to cross the street
Focus on the boy’s kindness and empathy toward the elderly woman
Know the meaning of the title 'Somebody’s Mother'
Remember the moral: small acts of kindness can make a big difference
